tags.tag: Chapter separator aphorism: "Elom sees all. Elom judges none." Frost continues to probe Marnette's faith. She feels watched and judged by his questions, even as the aphorism promises judgment never comes. Ironic tension builds.
Arrival at salve platform; contrasts 'home' with new unease.
Mundane announcement forces her to disembark.
Sensory reset of salve district; time-away felt huge.
Corridor walk and memorial wall deepen caste/lifespan worldbuilding.
Quarters + twin Elom carvings show faith now split/fragile.
Unpacking recalls Project Mayfly facts; highlights danger of evidence.
Lena's arrival reintroduces friendship and normal life.
Warm reunion establishes emotional anchor (and what's at risk).
Marnette can't fully re-enter normal; hints abrupt ending.
Lena reports Frost's death and protocol; notes Marnette's change.
Marnette deflects with 'tired' to hide turmoil.
Lena's awe at 793 years spotlights inequality; offers support.
Marnette's inner conflict: truth vs protecting Lena (and herself).
Marnette frames Frost's revelations as 'strange' dying talk.
Lena presses for details, raising the tension.
Marnette doubles down on the safe lie; mask stays on.
Lena normalizes delirium; pushes 'rest and return' narrative.
'Normal' becomes alien; theme of irreversible awakening.
Invitation to funeral service moves her into public faith-test.
Marnette agrees despite dread; commits to play the role.
Packed church and founding mosaics show collective reverence.
Name-slip revelation: Elon vs Elom clicks into place.
Sanctuary now feels staged; Marnette's alienation sharpened.
Familiar faces underline her secret separation from the community.
Priest Malachi introduced as authority; his age echoes lifespan limits.
Sermon opens, sanctifying Frost and the colony's mythology.
Marnette reacts to 'Elom himself'; doubts intensify.
Ritual prayer begins; conformity pressure rises.
Automatic prayer shows conditioning and habit.
Prayer reinterpreted as engineering/caste control; poison in the words.
She can't 'close her eyes' anymore; isolation and envy of believers.
Sermon frames official story of Master death and mourning.
Malachi praises Frost's Archive, planting the later objective.
Key omissions (vote/Mayfly) reveal propaganda and suppression.
Doctrine reframes short lives as purposeful 'gift'.
Marnette recalls programmed cell death; fear of genetic kill-switch.
Congregation chants; she mimics silently, emotionally detached.
Malachi contrasts longevity vs purpose; repeats control doctrine.
Doctrine now feels like chains; Elena/grandmother memories sting.
She 'goes through motions'; hollowed-out faith made visible.
Lena notices missing hymn; gentle suspicion/concern.
Marnette's 'tired' becomes practiced shield.
Lena's empathy shows misunderstanding: grief vs worldview collapse.
Inner note: rest can't fix a shattered cosmology.
Marnette thanks Lena to preserve the relationship.
Parting shot: familiar corridors now feel like a cage.
Montage of normalcy-performance; recovery leave as cover.
Confession of incapacity to care again; faith damage stated.
Busywork shows avoidance of solitude and questions.
Dining hall scene restores social texture; funeral chatter continues.
Lena introduces Thomas as a new complication.
Thomas recalled as question-asker; foreshadows inquiry plotline.
Marnette probes his intent, cautious and controlled.
Lena details his growing doubts and dangerous questions.
Irony: Thomas mirrors Marnette's doubts, minus the evidence.
Marnette checks spread/exposure risk (practical paranoia).
Great Optimization lore appears; hints history has been edited.
Marnette asks what Lena wants, spotlighting moral pressure.
Lena asks for 'peace/acceptance' intervention; reveals fear of dissent.
Marnette recognizes her own isolation; can't be the old comforter.
She agrees to talk but won't promise a cure; boundary set.
Thomas's respect for her establishes leverage and stakes.
Memory of boy Thomas shows lifelong curiosity and connection.
Thomas alone in break room; mood and his changed maturity set.
Marnette opens the conversation; takes the risk.
Thomas is guarded; shows social exile has hardened him.
Marnette invites explanation, keeping the door open.
Thomas defines 'doubter' stigma; social cost made explicit.
Marnette acknowledges Lena's worry; a soft bridge.
Thomas's bitterness shows family pressure and reputational fear.
Marnette's pulse spikes at Frost mention; danger signal.
Thomas asks about founders/intent; drives plot toward truth.
Marnette faces lie-vs-truth fork; stakes for both.
She hedges with 'dying ramblings'; buys time, avoids confession.
Thomas presses on lifespan disparity; escalation.
His youth framed against looming death; urgency sharpened.
Marnette warns truth-seeking is dangerous; tests resolve.
Thomas insists on searching; defines him as committed dissenter.
Marnette recognizes herself in him; alliance potential forms.
She gives the honest advice: keep looking, be careful, distrust easy answers.
Narration marks her choice: honesty over comfort (turning point).
Nighttime loop of Frost's last words; guilt and obsession.
Promise weight grows; mission becomes unavoidable.
Attempt at real prayer sets up the breaking moment.
Prayer starts, then stalls; fracture dramatized.
She can't continue; faith language is corrupted now.
Doctrine translation #1: 'designed' becomes engineers, not divinity.
Doctrine translation #2: 'roles' become voted biological castes.
Doctrine translation #3: 'designated time' becomes encoded death sentence.
Prayer turns into address to Frost's memory; grief + obligation.
She names the practical obstacle: Archive is locked behind Master property.
No answers from the dead; the problem demands action.
Funeral spectacle contrasts public myth with private reality.
Display image of Frost in Archive triggers opportunity-thinking.
Caption emphasizes 'personal archive' as the key resource.
Reminder: she had access/clearance once; opportunity rekindled.
Core question reframed: access, not existence, is the hurdle.
Residence 7B logistics after death define a time window.
Archive likely preserved during mourning; stakes of timing.
Barriers listed: no authority, no cover; tension rises.
Single-word pivot: 'Unless' seeds solution.
Elara introduced as potential ally; past warning recontextualized.
Elara-as-helper stated plainly; plan target locked.
Risks of involving others weighed; paranoia justified.
Need declared: she can't do it alone; Elara singled out.
Decision hardens into plan: contact Elara, return, access Archive.
'And then what?' opens moral and strategic uncertainty.
'Tell them' question: who is them? contrasts Lena vs Thomas responses.
Re-centers on first step: see the evidence; momentum.
Six weeks later dawn clarity signals regained agency.
Mirror beat shows internal change beneath same face.
Declaration of 'Today' begins action phase.
She accepts uncertainty but rejects continued pretending.
Archive framed as world-changing; she's ready to question openly.
Prayer beads choice shows complicated faith, not simple rejection.
She physically moves toward transit; action replaces rumination.
She accepts consequences; ignorance no longer an option.
Boards transport; threshold crossed while district stays oblivious.
Travel observation mixes fear and hope; emotional tone set.
Frost's last words recur as mission mantra.
She answers aloud, reaffirming the promise.
Mars/Archive imagery widens scope and forebodes danger.
Closing line cements transformation: she's ready to look.
